description: Tests ambiguous responses across multiple phases: maybe, conditional yes, future intent, budget-as-decline, third-party answers, contextless words. Based on patterns across all four real conversation threads.

messages.body: As long as I don't have an exam either of the dates work
messages._note: CONDITIONAL YES: not a firm confirmation
CONTEXTLESS WORD: just a last name, no meaning in trip context. Should be ignored

messages.body: He has laundry scheduled for Nov 14 :/
messages._note: THIRD-PARTY SARCASTIC: talking about someone else sarcastically. Should not be parsed as anything meaningful

messages.body: You are 100% going to try and make me look like a villain on this trip

messages._note: AMBIGUOUS COMMIT: not yes, not no. Agent should ask for firm answer

messages.body: Damn this crew look fun but I'm also out bc skiing is expensive and my ass broke broke
messages._note: BUDGET AS DECLINE: this is an opt-out expressed through budget concern, not a budget poll response

messages.body: (Which might help me eventually not be broke broke)

messages._note: Status should show: 3 committed (Caroline, Noah, Matty), 1 ambiguous (Andy), 1 opted out (Amy)
